intro: AI is normalizing the corporate headshot as a text prompt, while it also folds quietly into professional retouching pipelines and drives viral portrait aesthetics that reset what clients expect a portrait to look like and cost.

trends.title: AI headshot generators normalize for LinkedIn and job hunting
trends.body: Services like HeadshotPro, Aragon AI, InstaHeadshots, and PhotoPacksAI produce a set of professional-looking headshots in minutes for under $50. Adoption skews Gen Z and millennial, and Canva has folded the feature into its own product.

trends.title: AI retouching becomes the default pro workflow, not a replacement
trends.body: Aftershoot's AI Retouching (skin texture, blemish removal, glare, object removal) shipped free through September 2025 before moving to paid plans, and by its 2026 update markets itself as a full cull-edit-retouch-deliver pipeline for working portrait photographers.

trends.title: Viral consumer AI portraits reset the aesthetic bar
trends.body: Google's Gemini 2.5 Flash Image, nicknamed Nano Banana, fueled the 3D-figurine selfie trend and the Bollywood-style vintage saree portrait trend across Instagram in September 2025, on the strength of unusually consistent face rendering.

trends.title: Traditional portrait work repositions upmarket
trends.body: General industry commentary frames the open question as whether traditional headshot photography gets relegated to executive portraits and special occasions, as everyday headshot demand gets absorbed by generators.

tools.name: Aftershoot
tools.note: AI culling, editing, and retouching for working photographers

tools.name: Google Gemini (Nano Banana)
tools.note: Conversational image editing behind the viral portrait trends

campaigns.title: The AI headshot did not start with a brand, it started with candidates
campaigns.body: Unlike the other four categories, portrait's clearest AI-adoption story is not a brand campaign but a labor-market one: individual job seekers adopting AI headshots faster than any studio or platform pushed them to.

controversies.title: Authenticity in hiring
controversies.body: Recruiters flag overly smooth AI portraits as a deception risk, worried the photo no longer represents how the candidate actually looks in person.

controversies.title: Likeness and privacy leakage
controversies.body: During the Nano Banana saree trend, one user reported a generated image reproducing a real mole not visible in their source photo. Google called the identifying marker coincidental.

controversies.title: The clearest labor displacement story of the five categories
controversies.body: Entry-level headshot work is the most directly substituted segment across all five categories studied here, a pattern the 2026 Zenfolio industry survey (58% of photographers report losing work to generative AI) backs with hard numbers.
